template: My faction is pushing east; we expect new territory before the next moon. Eyes are forward, hands are ready.
We are at war. Half my kin are wounded; the rest are sharpening blades. Sleep finds none of us.
We lost the last skirmish. I'm rationing my own training to feed the rebuild — it'll be a season before we move again.
We're courting an alliance. My cousin walks as envoy now; she sends word every third day.
We're holding what we have. No new fronts until the elders agree, and the elders take their time.
We've withdrawn from the field. The silence is by choice. Don't ask whose.
My faction is between phases. I keep my eyes open and my count low.
